source.title: Well Control Incident - Seadrill West Neptune, SS003 Well, MC Block 727

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 727

event.top_event: Well Control Incident - Unintentional Shearing of Fluid Loss Control Devices
actions_taken: Fluid loss pills spotted to reduce losses
Well shut in with annular and pipe ram
Circulated using driller's method kill procedure
Bullheading kill weight fluid down annulus and work string
Well successfully killed via bullheading with calcium carbonate pills
Well circulated on choke using driller's well control method
event.summary: On January 9, 2021, at approximately 06:00 hours, Kosmos Energy experienced a well control incident on Seadrill West Neptune during completion operations on the SS003 well. While displacing wellbore completion fluid to packer fluid, the downhole Equivalent Circulating Density (ECD) unintentionally sheared the upper and lower Fluid Loss Control Devices (FLCD), resulting in cross flow between completed intervals and fluid losses of 600 barrels per hour. The well was subsequently shut in and killed over a multi-day operation. The incident was attributed to inadequate risk recognition, poor communication, insufficient execution and supervision, and incomplete procedures.
recommendations: Improve risk assessment during planning and review phases of completion operations
Enhance procedural development to address risks associated with fluid loss control devices
Implement robust line-by-line procedural review and Complete Well on Paper (CWOP) processes
Establish direct communication protocols between Day Drilling Supervisor and Lead Drilling Supervisor
Require on-site presence of Halliburton Tool Representative and Consultant Completion Specialist during critical operations
Include specific pump rate and pump pressure parameters in displacement procedures
Implement Stop Work procedures when critical information is not provided
Enhance supervision and direct instruction during critical drilling operations
Develop detailed Plan of Action (POA) with measurable parameters for critical steps

hazards.name: High pressure fluid circulation during completion operations

threats.name: Excessive Equivalent Circulating Density (ECD) during packer fluid displacement
Uncontrolled pump rate during displacement operation
Inadequate supervision and communication during critical operation
Lack of specific pump rate and pressure parameters in procedures

consequences.name: Shearing and opening of upper and lower Fluid Loss Control Devices
Fluid losses of 600 barrels per hour
Well control incident requiring multi-day kill operations

controls.name: Risk Assessment During Planning and Review Phases
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate risks associated with fluid loss control device shearing
controls.lod_basis: Procedural development and line-by-line review should identify risks before execution

supporting_text: The risk, consequences, and mitigations associated with the inadvertent shearing and opening of the fluid loss control devices were not adequately assessed during the planning and review phases of the completion.

Within the Kosmos Management System, there are opportunities for this risk to be addressed during the procedural development phase and the line-by-line procedural review, as well as during the Complete Well on Paper (CWOP).

controls.name: Detailed Procedure with Pump Rate and Pressure Parameters
controls.barrier_role: Provide specific measurable parameters to prevent excessive ECD during displacement
controls.lod_basis: Procedures should specify pump rate and pressure limits to prevent FLCD shearing

supporting_text: The procedural step for the packer fluid displacement was written without mention of pump rate or pump pressure.

A note warning excessive pressure applied to the fluid loss control valves could cause them to shear and open was made in the procedure, but no direct measurable means of how to accomplish this step were provided.

controls.name: Direct Communication Between Supervisors
controls.barrier_role: Ensure clear and unambiguous instructions are communicated to the driller
controls.lod_basis: Direct communication prevents misinterpretation of critical operational instructions

supporting_text: Direct communication between the Day Drilling Supervisor and the Lead Drilling Supervisor did not take place even though both supervisors were aware of the Driller's need for understanding what pump rate to use.

The instruction to the Driller was given by the Lead Drilling Supervisor and the Halliburton Tool Rep. The Driller communicated the instructions to the Day Drilling Supervisor and confirmed the instruction came from the Lead Drilling Supervisor.

controls.name: On-Site Supervision by Specialist Personnel
controls.barrier_role: Provide expert oversight and guidance during critical displacement operations
controls.lod_basis: Specialist presence on rig floor enables real-time monitoring and intervention

supporting_text: The Halliburton Tool Rep and the Consultant Completion Specialist both failed to communicate information they should have known. Neither the Halliburton Tool Rep nor the Consultant Completion Specialist went to the rig floor to assist with supervising the displacement operation.
The Halliburton Tool Rep and the Consultant Completion Specialist were not on the rig floor during the displacement.

controls.name: Direct Supervision and Instruction During Critical Operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure driller receives direct supervision and clear instructions during packer fluid displacement
controls.lod_basis: Direct supervision prevents unsupervised execution of critical operations

supporting_text: There was no direct instruction or supervision given to the Driller during the packer fluid displacement.

The Day Drilling Supervisor was on the rig floor, but despite making an effort to get the pump rate information, he did not carry through with the task. He also did not give attention to the Driller and work scope as the Driller began the task for packer fluid displacement.
The Lead Drilling Supervisor had just sat down to dinner at the time of the incident.

controls.name: Stop Work Procedure When Critical Information Missing
controls.barrier_role: Halt operations when essential operational parameters are not provided
controls.lod_basis: Stop Work prevents execution of operations with incomplete information

supporting_text: Stop Work was not initiated when the information (pump rate) was not provided to the Driller.

controls.name: Wellhead Pressure Monitoring and Control
controls.barrier_role: Monitor and manage wellhead pressure to detect and control well control situations
controls.lod_basis: Pressure monitoring enables detection of well control and application of kill procedures

supporting_text: At approximately 17:00 hours on January 12, 2021, after losses stopped, the rig experienced approximately a 14-barrel gain on the trip tank.
While waiting on additional fluids and materials, wellhead pressure was managed by bullheading kill weight fluid when required.
At 14:30 hours on January 14, 2021, the pressure on the wellhead was 5839 psi

controls.name: Well Shut-In and Kill Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Isolate well and apply kill procedures to regain control
controls.lod_basis: Shut-in and kill procedures are standard well control mitigation measures

supporting_text: The well was shut in with one annular and one pipe ram closed and circulated out using the driller's method kill procedure.

Bullhead kill operations began at 18:00 hours on January 15, 2021, consisting of pumping kill weight fluid down the annulus, then down the drill pipe followed by several calcium carbonate fluid loss pill, the well was successfully killed.

controls.name: Fluid Loss Mitigation Pills
controls.barrier_role: Reduce fluid losses and stabilize well conditions
controls.lod_basis: Fluid loss pills are standard mitigation for loss of circulation events

supporting_text: A 50-barrel fluid loss pill was spotted, and losses slowed to 345 bhp. A second fluid loss pill was pumped which significantly decreased the losses eventually resulting in zero losses.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
